name: Qingle Liu
affiliation: Department of Computer Science and Technology, Tsinghua University, Beijing, China

entries.title: Tsinghua University
entries.subtitle: B.Eng. in CS & B.A. in Economics and Finance (Double Major)
bullets: **Core Coursework:** Calculus (A/A-), Linear Algebra (A), Advanced Linear Algebra (A+), University Physics (A+/A), Discrete Mathematics (A-), Fundamentals of Programming (A), Object-Oriented Programming (A), Programming Training (A), Data Structures (A-), Intro to Computer Systems (A)
**In Progress:** Probability & Mathematical Statistics, Intro to Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning & Financial Data Analysis, Intro to Machine Learning, Software Engineering

entries.title: National Scholarship
entries.subtitle: Top national undergraduate award in China

entries.title: Outstanding Communist Youth League Member, Tsinghua University
Institute of HCI and Media Integration, Tsinghua University
entries.subtitle: Undergraduate Researcher, advised by Academician Shimin Hu
bullets: Conducting research on multimodal video understanding and GUI agents.
Two papers under review at **ECCV 2026** (not yet on arXiv):
*Bridging VideoQA and Video-Guided Agentic Tasks via Generalized Keyframe Extraction* (2nd author)
*GUICrafter: Weakly-Supervised GUI Agent Leveraging Massive Unannotated Screenshots* (4th author)

entries.title: Einsia.AI
entries.subtitle: Software Developer & Research Engineer
bullets: **Dev Team -- Deep Research:** Contributed to the development of Einsia's [Deep Research](https://einsia.ai/deep-research/) product, an AI-powered academic research agent.

**Lab Team -- Frontier-Eng:** Participated in the *Frontier-Eng* project, a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on real-world engineering optimization tasks (47 tasks, 5 categories). Paper: [arXiv:2604.12290](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12290).
**Lab Team -- AI4Health:** Currently contributing to the AI for Health project (ongoing).

entries.title: Optical-Chip Sign Language Translation Glove
entries.subtitle: Algorithm Design & Model Training -- SRT Program
bullets: Participating in the National College Student Innovation Competition; funded as an SRT (Student Research Training) project at Tsinghua University.
Responsible for algorithm design and model training for a sign language translation glove based on an optical chip sensor.

entries.title: Class President, Dept. of Computer Science and Technology
